channel.title: dubrovinpe.com — Web Building & AI
channel.description: Exploring the intersection of modern web development and artificial intelligence.

item.title: Why AI Will Not Replace Web Developers — But Will Change Everything Else
item.description: The discourse around AI and web development is trapped between utopian promises and existential dread. The truth is more nuanced.

item.title: The Quiet Power of Semantic HTML in an AI-Driven World
item.description: As language models increasingly consume and interpret the web, the semantic structure of your markup becomes a competitive advantage.

item.title: Design Tokens at Scale: Building Systems That Outlast Frameworks
item.description: Frameworks come and go. A well-architected design token system survives them all.

item.title: From ChatGPT to Production: A Practical Guide to LLM Integration
item.description: Everyone has played with ChatGPT. Few have shipped a product powered by an LLM.

item.title: CSS Has Grown Up: Container Queries, Layers, and the End of the Cascade Wars
item.description: Modern CSS solves the problems we spent a decade working around with JavaScript.

item.title: The Minimal Viable Website: Why Your Side Project Does Not Need React
item.description: Not every website needs a framework. Sometimes, a well-crafted HTML file is the most powerful thing you can ship.

item.title: Prompt Engineering Is Just Programming in a Different Language
item.description: The discourse around prompt engineering treats it as a new discipline. It is not. It is programming with natural language.

item.title: The Component Is Not the Unit of Design
item.description: We organize our codebases around components, but users experience patterns.

item.title: Why I Still Write CSS by Hand (and You Should Too)
item.description: In the age of Tailwind and CSS-in-JS, hand-written CSS seems anachronistic. Here is why it remains the most powerful way to think about visual design.

item.title: Neural Networks for Frontend Developers: A Gentle Introduction
item.description: You do not need a PhD to understand neural networks. You need the right mental models.
